sections.title: Qualification requirements
sections.body:
.  SLO, GA or fractional ops performance. Only Commercial performance factors shall be applied.

-  PF shall hold a current AHQ.
-  For DEPARTURE RWY26, the pilot flying shall be a qualified EDFE PIC.
-  The PIC must not be designated as ‘INEXPERIENCED’.
-  TCAS and EGPWS must be operative (ie no MEL).

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
he aerodrome is located 11NM south of the city of Frankfurt and 5NM south of Frankfurt-Main International airport
(EDDF).  MSA within 25NM of the ARP is 4000ft.

OVERVIEW FROM THE SOUTHWEST

lines: he aerodrome is located 11NM south of the city of Frankfurt and 5NM south of Frankfurt-Main International airport
(EDDF).  MSA within 25NM of the ARP is 4000ft.

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
HREATS:
RISK OF COLLISION.
There is no dedicated approach to this Airport.  LANGEN RADAR /FRANKFURT APP will control you to their minimum
Radar Vectored Altitude then expect you to cancel IFR and continue visually to EDFE below the Frankfurt Class C
Airspace.  This will place you in the equivalent of Class E/G airspace during the final stages of approach or initial
stages of departure.  Crew must expect uncontrolled traffic in the vicinity of EDFE.  Adhere to OM-A 8.3.1 VFR Policy
and crew may find additional guidance on uncontrolled airspace operations in:  COMPLY/200-FLEET Training &
Standards/250-FLEET Training & Standards Library/IFR/VFR Ops in Class E & G Airspace (UK/EU) Enrichment
Guide.

CONTROLLED AIRSPACE:
EDDF is close to EDFE and defines the arrival and departure procedures available.  EDFE EGELSBACH has an
ATZ/RMZ/TMZ Class G (SFC/1500ft) beneath the EDDF Class C FRANKFURT A area (2500ft – FL100) and the
FRANKFURT CLASS D (1500ft -2500ft) area.  The extended centreline of RWY 18 is less than 3NM away.  Crew may
expect a stepped descent below this altitude with LANGEN RADAR and will be required to cancel IFR prior to the
lateral limit of the FRANKFURT ZONE.  Review the map on the FD Pro App in VFR mode with airspace ON.

OBSTACLES ON RWY 26 APPROACH.
A railway with power lines runs across the approach path approximately 900 feet before the displaced threshold.
The height of the power lines is 32 feet, these obstacles are lit and marked with balloons alternating white and red.
An Abbreviated two-light Precision Approach Path Indicator (APAPI) set at 4.4° and is the only vertical guidance for
approach and landing.  Egelsbach AIP strongly recommends crews follow the APAPI during final approach to ensure
safe clearance of the powerlines.

FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH, GERMANY
FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH
30-10-2P3

-
Service is provided by EGELSBACH INFORMATION, (Air to Ground Station).   EGELSBACH APRON provides start-up and
clearance (frequencies as per EDFE Jepp Chart 39-2).
LANGEN INFORMATION provides an FIS (Jepp Chart 39-1A).

The following NETJETS TAILORED CHARTS exist:
30-3-NJE:
Netjets VFR Departure.
39-10-NJE:
Netjets Visual Apch Rwy26 via ASBAB.

REQUIRED WAYPOINTS
Check if coordinates are already in the FMS (or enter manually).
MAX 160KIAS at WPTs.

WPT
LAT/LONG
SPESA
N49 51.7  E009 20.9
PEKIG
N49 59.6  E009 03.1
ASBAB
N49 58.1  E008 50.9
DELTA
N49 52.2  E008 36.1
D3
N49 55.3  E008 36.7
YANKEE (Insert ‘Y’ in FMS)
N49 58.2  E008 51.4
Note: point Y is within 0.3NM of point ASBAB

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1 GENERAL
THREATS
Only RWY 26 is approved for arrival due to the proximity of EDDF.   The German ATC Service (DFS) has a procedure
for arrivals to RWY 26 at EDFE.
Expect to be handed to LANGEN RADAR for descent.  LANGEN will provide RADAR VECTORS via PEKIG and ASBAB to
ease pressure on FRANKFURT departures - this procedure REPLACES all other previous arrivals to EDFE.  In certain
RWY configurations at EDDF a handover to FRANFURT APPROACH may be arranged by LANGEN.
iPad:  Consider use of VFR map, with Roads and Airspace selected - this will aid situational awareness.

CAUTION:
ASBAB is just within the Frankfurt Class C airspace and 0.3nm beyond compulsory reporting point YANKEE (Y).  A
VFR arrival via YANKEE is possible, Point YANKEE is within 0.3 NM of ASBAB.

Arrival YANKEE (Y) routing is defined as:
YANKEE (Y)-(VFR 1400')-RWY26

FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH, GERMANY
FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH
30-10-2P4

-

OVERLAY ON RADAR MINIMUMS

RWY 26 - NOTE RAILWAY & POWERLINES IN UNDERSHOOT. DISPLACED THRESHOLD.

FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH, GERMANY
FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH
30-10-2P5

-
9.2  ARRIVAL RWY26 VIA PEKIG - ASBAB

MINIMA:

DAY ONLY

Visibility 8KM,  Ceiling 1900ft (2300Ft QNH).

PERFORMANCE:
As per PDCS.  Restrict arrival weight to not more than departure DP26 to allow for the balked
landing scenario following the motorway to the south.

PROCEDURE:
Request RADAR VECTORS TO PEKIG with LANGEN.  They are familiar with this routing.
Enter the following route into the FMS.  Allow enough time to do this:

PEKIG (3000ft ) – ASBAB (2100ft / winter 2300ft) – EDFE RWY 26.
ASBAB should be considered the MAP for this approach.
Contact EGELSBACH INFORMATION for the latest airfield information and maintain a listening
watch for traffic on secondary VHF no LATER THAN 10 MINUTES PRIOR TO ARRIVAL (AIP
requirement).
LANGEN RADAR may offer vectors to intercept final approach track via ASBAB (course 265°).
When EDDF is on WESTERLY arrivals (RWY 25L/C/R), expect an initial routing to PEKIG via an
EDDF RNAV STAR or RNAV Transition point.  The relevant EDDF RNAV arrival charts will aid
situational awareness.  KERAX, SPESA and INBOS are likely.
ATC may require holding at CHA VOR. This holding pattern is displayed on some of the EDDF
Jepp 10-2 series charts or EDFE 39-10-NJE.
CANCELLING IFR:
The change to VFR must take place ‘prior to ASBAB’ (instruction from DFS).  If this is not
possible follow the MISSED APPROACH instructions below.
DESCENT PROFILE:
From ASBAB at 2100ft QNH (1700ft AAL) descend to 1500ft to enter the EDFE RMZ at or below
1500ft (5NM from THOLD RWY 26).
Reduce the rate of descent to intercept and then follow the APAPI for a 4.4° glidepath.  DO
NOT fly a shallow glidepath due to the Displaced THOLD, powerlines and Railway on SHORT
FINAL.
Be aware that at ASBAB you may not have the RWY in sight at the 8KM minimum visibility, but
if clear of cloud and in sight of the ground the visual approach and descent may be continued.
CONTINGENCY:
PDCS STRAIGHT  & 26DP are available.

9.3  MISSED APPROACH - RWY26
This procedure is shown on Jepp 39-10-NJE.  If at ASBAB (7.7NM from RWY) you are:

• Unable to cancel IFR and continue VFR/VMC, or
• IFR cancellation is not confirmed by LANGEN RADAR or FRANKFURT APP.

THEN
• Follow any Instructions given by LANGEN RADAR.

IF NO INSTRUCTIONS RECEIVED:
•  Climb to 4000ft.
•  Initiate a LEFT climbing turn (minimum 15º AOB / MAX 160 KIAS),
•  Fly inbound CHA VOR,
•  Inform LANGEN RADAR or FRANKFURT APP at the earliest opportunity.

In case of lost communication, commence a left turn (MAX 160KIAS) inbound CHA and climb to 4000ft and attempt
to regain contact with LANGEN RADAR or FRANKFURT APP.

FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH, GERMANY
FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH
30-10-2P6

-
Enter published holding (refer to EDDF STAR/RNAV transition charts).
9.4  BALKED LANDING RWY26 AFTER CANCELLING IFR
This procedure is described on Jepp 39-10-NJE.
Maintain VMC.

• GREATER THAN 5NM (track distance) from THOLD: (ie at or above 1500ft QNH), follow 9.3 MISSED APPROACH-
RWY 26 above. Note the following:
-
Be aware that you will need to use FMS distance, there is no DME.
-
Contact LANGEN or FRANKFURT frequency as soon as safely possible – the procedure will take you into
the CLASS C FRANKFURT zone above 1500ft QNH.

•  DURING FINAL VISUAL DESCENT BELOW 1500ft:
-
Climb and maintain 1400ft and 160KIAS maximum.
-
Route: D3 – DELTA – FKS VOR/DME  (1400' / K160-).
-
Advise EGELSBACH INFO of your intentions.
-
If an additional approach is required, remain with EGELSBACH INFORMATION until advised, expect on
passing DELTA to regain IFR with LANGEN RADAR / FRANKFURT APP and be routed to PEKIG for RWY 26
(Entry YANKEE) arrival.
-
If an additional approach is not required, then remain with EGELSBACH INFORMATION until advised,
expect to regain IFR with LANGEN RADAR for further climb when passing DELTA.

Be aware that you will need to use FMS distance, there is no DME.
Contact LANGEN or FRANKFURT frequency as soon as safely possible – the procedure will take you into
the CLASS C FRANKFURT zone above 1500ft QNH.
Route: D3 – DELTA – FKS VOR/DME  (1400' / K160-).
Advise EGELSBACH INFO of your intentions.
If an additional approach is required, remain with EGELSBACH INFORMATION until advised, expect on
passing DELTA to regain IFR with LANGEN RADAR / FRANKFURT APP and be routed to PEKIG for RWY 26
If an additional approach is not required, then remain with EGELSBACH INFORMATION until advised,
expect to regain IFR with LANGEN RADAR for further climb when passing DELTA.

sections.title: Departure procedures
sections.body:
0.1 GENERAL
THREATS
Departures are allowed from both RWY 08 and RWY 26.
EGELSBACH INFORMATION will provide the departure instructions.
See Jeppesen EDFE 30-3-NJE Netjets VFR Departure.
Consider a listening watch with EDDF FRANKFURT TWR on secondary VHF (118.780 Mhz or 119.905 Mhz).

10.2  DEPARTURE RWY 08 (EXIT YANKEE)
ROUTE: YANKEE (Y) (1400' / K160-)

If able to maintain VMC, after YANKEE (Y) continue climb to 3400ft MSL. Further climb and routing must be
coordinated with LANGEN RADAR or FRANKFURT APP. YANKEE is a compulsory reporting point.

10.3  LOWER VISIBILITY DEPARTURE RWY 08 (COORDINATED VFR/IFR CHANGEOVER) (EXIT YANKEE)
MINIMA:

DAY ONLY, RWY 08 ONLY.  Visibility 5KM and clear of cloud until IFR.

PERFORMANCE:
As per PDCS.

PROCEDURE:
ROUTE: YANKEE (Y) (1400')
EGELSBACH INFORMATION will relay ATC clearance from LANGEN RADAR, including routing,
airborne frequency, and squawk code.
Expect to receive departure slot (clearance valid from ... To ...) or instruction to call for release
by LANGEN RADAR.
IFR starts passing MRVA, as stated in the clearance.

CONTINGENCY:
Straight Procedure only from RWY 08.

FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH, GERMANY
FRANKFURT-EGELSBACH
30-10-2P7

-
10.4  DEPARTURE RWY 26 (EXIT DELTA)
MINIMA:

DAY ONLY, visibility 8KM, Ceiling 1900ft, clear of cloud until IFR.

PERFORMANCE:
As per PDCS.  Use 26DP.

PROCEDURE:
At 785ft (400AGL) turn LEFT to D3

ROUTE: D3 – DELTA – FKS (VOR/DME) .  level and maintain 1400ft and 160KIAS maximum.
Avoid Erzhausen.
Remain WEST of the A5 Motorway but stay within 0.5Nm to avoid EDDF CTR (Class D SFC -
1500ft).
DELTA is a compulsory Reporting Point.

CONTINGENCY:
Straight Procedure only from RWY 08.

DEPARTURE RWY 26 - AVOID ERZHAUSEN (NOISE SENSITIVE)

sections.body:
ore information about Frankfurt-Egelsbach, including a WEBCAM for a weather preview, can be found on
http://www.egelsbach-airport.com
Standard VFR Squawk in Germany is 7000.

sections.title: Changes since the last revision
sections.body:
ew DFS procedure for arrival RWY 26.  RID VOR/DME replaced with FKS VOR/DME. Corrections to
procedure to perform departures from RWY 26. Airfield Call Sign change from Egelsbach Radio -> Egelsbach
Information.
Provide feedback or address any questions to Lis-OpsTechnical@netjets.com
